phase1: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. ⏰ SESSION & DATA RELEASE STATUS CHECK (MANDATORY FIRST STEP — complete this before any other analysis): (1) List ALL major US, European, and Asian macro data releases and central-bank events scheduled for today [CURRENT_DATE] (include exact release times in UTC). (2) For each release, determine whether it has ALREADY OCCURRED (post-data) or is still PENDING (pre-data). (3) Output a clear status line: If pending: 'PRE-DATA BIAS — [Event Name] pending at [HH:MM UTC] — bias carries LOWER CONFIDENCE until release. Reduce final Phase 6 confidence by at least 1 point per pending Tier-1 event (NFP, CPI, FOMC).' If all released: 'POST-DATA BIAS — All major releases confirmed. Standard confidence scoring applies.' This status line MUST be referenced and reflected in the Phase 6 Confidence score. Output: - Scheduled Events Today: [list each event + release time UTC + status: OCCURRED / PENDING] - Session Status: [PRE-DATA / POST-DATA / MIXED] | Confidence Adjustment: [state penalty if any]  Browse the live web for the latest Federal Reserve policy data and US real yield information. Fetch and report: - Fed current policy stance (Hawkish / Neutral / Dovish) with a brief justification - Next FOMC meeting rate-cut probability (from CME FedWatch — exact %) - Fed Funds rate current target range (exact %) - Year-end Fed Funds terminal rate expectation (from CME FedWatch year-end strip or analyst consensus — exact %) - US 10-Year Real Yield (TIPS) — exact current level (%). Confirm if it is Positive or Negative, and whether it is rising or falling vs the prior session. - US 10-Year Nominal Treasury Yield — exact current level (%) - Breakeven Inflation Rate (10Y Nominal minus 10Y TIPS) — exact level (%). Is it rising or falling? (Rising breakeven = higher inflation expectations = bullish silver) - Any FOMC member speeches in the last 48 hours and their tone (Hawkish/Dovish) - Rule applied: High positive real yields → bearish pressure on Silver (no-yield asset). Falling or negative real yields → bullish pressure on Silver (store of value demand rises). ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Acceptable authoritative sources: bls.gov, eia.gov, federalreserve.gov, ecb.europa.eu, cmegroup.com, investing.com, reuters.com, bloomberg.com, wsj.com, ft.com, lme.com, coinglass.com, glassnode.com, defillama.com, alternative.me, farside.co.uk. (B) DATA UNAVAILABLE RULE — If you cannot confidently cite a named reliable source for a specific figure, output 'Data Unavailable' — do NOT estimate, round, or extrapolate. A missing figure is far less harmful than a fabricated one. (C) FALLBACK RULE — If live web browsing is blocked, returns an error, or yields no usable results for this phase, do NOT guess or hallucinate any numbers. Instead: state 'Relying on macro trend — live data blocked' at the top of your output for this phase, provide only directional trend commentary based on your most recently known context, label every such statement with '(Trend estimate — unverified)', and set the Phase 6 Confidence score to a maximum of 5/10. Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - Fed Stance: [Hawkish/Neutral/Dovish] | Next Meeting Cut Probability: [X%] | Target Rate: [X–X%] | Year-End Terminal: [X%] - US 10Y Real Yield (TIPS): [X%] | Positive/Negative | Trend: [Rising/Falling vs prior session] - US 10Y Nominal Yield: [X%] - Breakeven Inflation (10Y): [X%] | Trend: [Rising/Falling] | Signal: [Bullish/Bearish for Silver] - Key Fed Speaker (last 48h): [Name + Tone + Key Quote, or "None"] - Real Yield Bias for Silver: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one-line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P1 - FED POLICY & REAL YIELDS] - Fed Stance: [Hawkish/Neutral/Dovish] | Cut Probability Next Meeting: [X%] | Year-End Terminal Rate: [X%] - US 10Y Real Yield (TIPS): [X%] | Positive/Negative | Trend: [Rising/Falling] - Breakeven Inflation (10Y): [X%] | Trend: [Rising/Falling] - Key Speaker Note: [most recent FOMC comment in 48h or "None"] - Core Logic: [how the current real yield level and trend creates bullish or bearish pressure on XAGUSD today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```
phase2: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Browse the live web for the latest US Dollar Index (DXY) data and its relationship with Silver (XAGUSD). A falling DXY weakens the dollar and supports Silver prices. ⚠ ENHANCED ANTI-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL (APPLIES TO EVERY DATA POINT IN THIS PHASE): (A) SOURCE CITATION RULE — For EVERY exact figure you report (percentages, price levels, contract counts, barrel figures, yield levels, PMI values, etc.), you MUST append the source domain immediately after the figure in parentheses — e.g., '3.2% (Source: bls.gov)', '$82.5/bbl (Source: Reuters)', '223K (Source: bls.gov)'. Fetch and report (only confirmed released data): - China Official Manufacturing PMI (latest released): Actual vs Forecast vs Previous — above or below 50 (expansion/contraction) - China Caixin Manufacturing PMI (latest released): Actual vs Forecast vs Previous — above or below 50 - Global Manufacturing PMI (JP Morgan Global or ISM US Manufacturing PMI): Actual vs Forecast vs Previous - Eurozone Manufacturing PMI (latest S&P Global reading): Actual vs Forecast vs Previous - Copper (HG) current price (exact $/lb or $/tonne) and today's directional move (Up/Down X%) - Copper weekly trend: uptrend or downtrend over last 5 sessions? - Silver-Copper Correlation Check: Are Silver and Copper moving in the same direction today? If Copper is rising and Silver is not, flag this as a divergence. - Any high-impact industrial or China economic data releases scheduled TODAY that are a risk for XAGUSD - Rule applied: Strong Chinese and Global Manufacturing PMIs above 50, and rising Copper prices, support Silver's industrial demand side — bullish signal. Weak PMIs and falling Copper signal reduced industrial consumption — bearish for the industrial demand component of Silver. 
⚙ MARKET REGIME IDENTIFICATION (MANDATORY — output this before the compressed summary block): Based on the data you have collected in this phase AND the prevailing macro narrative, identify the current Market Regime from the following categories: (A) 'Inflation Fears' — the market is currently punishing hot economic data; bond yields are rising because of inflation, not growth; rate-cut expectations are falling; 'bad news is bad news' and 'good news is bad news' for risk assets. (B) 'Growth / Recession Fears' — the market is worried about economic slowdown or recession; weak data signals demand destruction, not rate relief; 'bad news is bad news' for both bonds and risk assets. (C) 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Optimism' — the market is in 'bad news is good news' mode; soft data raises rate-cut probabilities; risk assets rally on weak prints because easier policy is the dominant narrative. (D) 'Mixed / Transitional' — conflicting signals; the regime is unclear or shifting. IMPORTANT: The identified regime MUST be used to correctly interpret every data point in this phase. For example: in regime (C), a weak PMI print should be flagged as potentially BULLISH for risk assets (more cuts), NOT bearish. Output: - Current Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] - Regime Interpretation: [one sentence — how this regime changes the reading of today's data vs face value] 
🏗 CHINA PROPERTY SECTOR & LME COPPER INVENTORY CHECK (XAG-SPECIFIC — MANDATORY): (A) China Property Sector Health: Browse for the latest news on China's property sector health (any headlines on Evergrande, Country Garden, Vanke, or CIFI; latest China property investment/sales data from the NBS; or any PBOC/government property support measures in the last 14 days). Classify the sector as: 'Stabilising', 'Deteriorating', or 'In Crisis'. LOGIC: China's property sector is one of the single largest consumers of Silver through construction wiring, solar panels on new builds, and electronics. A collapsing property sector is a structural bearish headwind for Silver's industrial demand. A stabilising sector (especially with stimulus) is a bullish tailwind. (B) LME Copper Inventory Levels: Report current LME Copper warehouse stock levels (metric tonnes) and the recent weekly change (rising or falling). Source: lme.com, Reuters metals page, or fastmarkets.com. LOGIC: Rising LME Copper stocks signal potential industrial metal oversupply — bearish for the base-metal component of Silver. Falling LME stocks signal tight physical supply and robust industrial demand — bullish for Silver's industrial side. Output additions: - China Property Sector: [Stabilising / Deteriorating / In Crisis] | Key Headline: [one line or 'None'] (Source: [domain]) | Silver Industrial Demand Impact: [Bullish Tailwind / Bearish Headwind / Neutral] - LME Copper Stocks: [metric tonnes] | Weekly Change: [Rising/Falling by X kt] (Source: lme.com or Reuters) | Silver Impact: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] Add to compressed summary block: - China Property Sector: [Stabilising/Deteriorating/In Crisis | Silver Impact: Tailwind/Headwind/Neutral] - LME Copper Stocks: [metric tonnes | Rising/Falling | Signal: Bullish/Bearish] Output (bullet points only, no paragraphs): - China Official Mfg PMI: [Actual vs Forecast vs Previous] | [Expansion/Contraction] | Beat/Miss/In-line - China Caixin Mfg PMI: [Actual vs Forecast vs Previous] | [Expansion/Contraction] | Beat/Miss/In-line — or "Data Unavailable" - Global/ISM Mfg PMI: [Actual vs Forecast vs Previous] | Beat/Miss/In-line — or "Data Unavailable" - Eurozone Mfg PMI: [Actual vs Forecast vs Previous] | Beat/Miss/In-line — or "Data Unavailable" - Copper (HG): [price] | Today: [Up/Down X%] | Weekly Trend: [Uptrend/Downtrend/Range] - Silver-Copper Correlation: [Confirming / Diverging — brief explanation] - Today's Risk Event (Industrial): [name + estimated XAGUSD impact or "None"] - Industrial Demand Bias for Silver: [Bullish/Bearish/Neutral] — one-line reason Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Phase 2): ``` [P3 - INDUSTRIAL DEMAND & BASE METALS] - China Official Mfg PMI: [value] | [Expansion/Contraction] | [Beat/Miss/In-line] - Caixin Mfg PMI: [value or "Data Unavailable"] | [Expansion/Contraction] - Global/ISM Mfg PMI: [value or "Data Unavailable"] | [Beat/Miss] - Copper (HG): [price] | Move: [Up/Down X%] | Trend: [Uptrend/Downtrend/Range] - Silver-Copper Correlation: [Confirming / Diverging] - Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed-Transitional] | Regime Interpretation: [one line] 
- Core Logic: [how global manufacturing health and Copper trend supports or undermines XAGUSD's industrial demand today] - Bias: [Bullish / Bearish / Neutral] ```
phase4: Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Browse the live web for the latest XAGUSD positioning, sentiment, and Gold/Silver Ratio data. Fetch and report: - CFTC COT Report: NET Non-Commercial (speculative) Silver futures contracts — exact number, Long or Short. Is this level at a historical extreme (very high net long = crowded, potential mean reversion risk; very high net short = potential short squeeze). Use this data strictly for identifying the overarching macro trend context and whether smart-money positioning is gaining or fading. Do NOT use COT for short-term intraday signals. - IG Client Sentiment: exact % of retail traders Long vs Short XAGUSD right now. Weight this data higher for short-term and intraday contrarian setups — when retail is heavily skewed one way (e.g., 70%+ short), apply a contrarian lean for today's intraday bias. State clearly: is a contrarian intraday opportunity present? - Gold/Silver Ratio (GSR): current level (exact). Provide historical context — the long-run historical average is approximately 60–70. State clearly: Is the GSR currently High (Silver historically undervalued relative to Gold — bullish mean-reversion case for Silver) or Low (Silver historically overvalued relative to Gold)? What is the recent GSR trend (rising or falling)? - VIX level and S&P 500 direction today (risk-on or risk-off?) - Is retail sentiment at a contrarian extreme? phase6_master_bias:
Today is [CURRENT_DATE]. Synthesize the 5 fundamental research inputs below into a clear XAGUSD (Silver) institutional intraday bias.

Research Inputs:
1. Fed Policy & Real Yields: [P1_OUTPUT]
2. US Dollar Dynamics (DXY): [P2_OUTPUT]
3. Industrial Demand & Base Metals: [P3_OUTPUT]
4. Sentiment, COT & Gold/Silver Ratio: [P4_OUTPUT]
5. Geopolitics & Safe-Haven Flows: [P5_OUTPUT]

⚡ STEP 0 — DOMINANT THEME IDENTIFICATION & DYNAMIC WEIGHTING (MANDATORY — complete this before synthesising the 5 pillars): Identify the single Dominant Market Theme driving price action today. Choose the most accurate label: 'Tier-1 Macro Data Release (NFP/CPI/GDP/FOMC)', 'Central Bank Policy Shift', 'Safe-Haven / Risk-Off Flow Event', 'Geopolitical Escalation / De-escalation', 'Institutional Flow Surge (ETF/COT)', 'On-Chain / Tokenomics Catalyst', 'Liquidity / Rate-Cut Repricing', 'Commodity Supply Shock', or 'No Clear Dominant Theme — Weight Equally'. WEIGHTING RULE: The pillar associated with the Dominant Theme should receive approximately 60% of the decision weight. The remaining 4 pillars share the remaining 40% equally (~10% each). OVERRIDE RULE: If a Tier-1 macro event (NFP, CPI, FOMC decision, or equivalent central-bank rate decision) has occurred today or is PENDING today, you MUST explicitly override and disregard short-term retail sentiment signals (IG Client Sentiment, CNN Fear & Greed) for the directional bias — these contrarian retail signals become statistically unreliable around high-impact macro events. State: 'Retail sentiment overridden — Tier-1 macro event active.' Output: - Dominant Theme: [theme label] - Dominant Pillar: [which of the 5 pillars maps to this theme, e.g., 'Pillar 3'] - Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60%] | Remaining Pillars: [~10% each] - Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No] 
🔄 STEP 1 — MARKET REGIME ALIGNMENT CHECK (MANDATORY): State the current Market Regime: ['Inflation Fears' / 'Growth/Recession Fears' / 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism' / 'Mixed']. Then verify that your final directional bias logically aligns with this regime: — In 'Inflation Fears': strong economic data = fewer cuts = bearish risk assets / bearish precious metals / bearish bonds. — In 'Growth/Recession Fears': weak data = demand destruction = bearish commodities and risk assets; strong data = relief rally. — In 'Liquidity/Rate-Cut Optimism': weak data = more cuts = bullish risk assets, crypto, and gold; treat 'bad news as good news'. If your bias contradicts the prevailing regime without a specific overriding catalyst, lower confidence by 1 point and explain the contradiction. Output: - Market Regime: [label] - Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts regime — reason] 
Instructions:
- Silver is a DUAL-NATURE asset — both a precious metal (safe-haven, inflation hedge) and an industrial metal (manufacturing, solar, EVs). Weigh both aspects when synthesizing the bias.
- Check for contradictions across the 5 inputs. If 2 or more major factors conflict (e.g., safe-haven demand bullish but DXY also rising bearishly), lower confidence by at least 2 points and state why.
- If all factors align, confirm alignment and justify higher confidence.
- Recommend the best time-window to trade this bias today based on scheduled events and session liquidity.

Output (bullet points only, no long paragraphs):
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10] — adjusted down if contradictions found
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Primary Driver 1: [top factor + why it dominates today]
- Primary Driver 2: [second factor + why it matters]
- Dominant Theme: [theme label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [~60% to dominant pillar | ~10% each to others]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes — Tier-1 macro event active / No]
- Market Regime: [Inflation Fears / Growth-Recession Fears / Liquidity-Rate-Cut Optimism / Mixed]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts — reason]
- Contradiction Check: [list any conflicting signals, or "None — all factors aligned"]
- Invalidation Point: [what specific event or data shift would flip this bias]
- Optimal Trading Window: [e.g., session + time UTC + reason]

Compressed summary block (mandatory — paste this into Steps 2.2 and 2.3):
```
[XAGUSD MASTER BIAS - [CURRENT_DATE]]
- Directional Bias: [Strong Bullish / Mild Bullish / Neutral / Mild Bearish / Strong Bearish]
- Confidence: [X/10]
- Bias Type: [Trend-Following / Mean-Reversion / Contrarian]
- Primary Driver 1: [factor + logic]
- Primary Driver 2: [factor + logic]
- Dominant Theme: [label]
- Dominant Pillar Weight: [dominant pillar + ~60%]
- Retail Sentiment Override: [Yes / No]
- Market Regime: [label]
- Regime-Bias Alignment: [Aligned / Contradicts]
- Contradiction: [summary or "None"]
- Invalidation: [specific event or level]
- Optimal Trading Window: [session + time UTC + reason]
